definitions: An antibiotic and antineoplastic agent, isolated from the bacterium Streptomyces achromogenes, used in the treatment of certain pancreatic cancers.
A naturally occurring chemical that is toxic to the beta cells of the pancreas, often used in medical research to induce diabetes in experimental animals.
examples: Research often involves streptozotocin to study diabetes.
The drug streptozotocin targets specific cells.
